content:
- Completed the audit task without product code changes because the required long-lane coverage was already present and behavior passed real verification.
- Updated the task file to completed/passes=true, ran task_switch.sh, committed the Ralph/task bookkeeping, and pushed commit 907679b.
- Next if resuming later: start from the new Ralph-selected task rather than this completed story-23 audit task.

- Read previous progress log for Choose-Task and loaded the current Ralph task summaries.
- Found two active failing tasks in current_tasks.md: story 21 task 07 and story 26 task 01. Story 26 is visibly blocked by story 23 task 01.
- Next I need to refresh the task summary, read the choose-task rules, inspect the two active task files for priority/blocking details, and confirm blocker completion against current_tasks_done before writing .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Refreshed Ralph summaries and read the choose-task instructions plus both active task files.
- Story 21 task 07 is an unblocked failing task but its own task definition says it is suspended and should be last in that story because it is out of scope for now.
- Story 26 task 01 is listed with blocked_by story 23 task 01; next I am confirming whether story 23 is already marked passes=true in current_tasks_done and doing a minimal repo-shape check so the selection reflects the actual stack.

- Confirmed the actual repo stack is a Rust workspace (runner, operator-log, ingest-contract, setup-sql) with make targets that also run a Go verifyservice test lane.
- Confirmed story 23 task 01 is completed with passes=true in current_tasks_done, so story 26 task 01 is no longer blocked by the blocker rule.
- Selected story 26 task 01 as the next task and wrote its path to .ralph/current_task.txt because it is an unblocked failing task, while story 21 task 07 is explicitly suspended/out-of-scope for now in its own task definition.

- Read previous Ralph progress and confirmed the new active task is story-26 task 01 about hosted GitHub workflow failures, image-build parallelism, and Quay security findings.
- Re-read the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skill instructions from disk so this turn follows the mandated workflow.
- Next: inspect the task markdown and determine whether it is still in planning/verification mode or ready for execution.

- Confirmed story-26 task has no existing plan and is unblocked, so this turn is a planning turn.
- Inspected the current publish workflow and prior Quay/security plans to identify the likely boundary cleanup: keep registry topology in the workflow contract and remove registry-host ownership from runtime artifact identity.
- Next: verify the latest hosted workflow evidence and then write the new story-26 plan file with either TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE as required.

- Used the authenticated GitHub API wrapper against djosh34/cockroach_migrate_tool and confirmed publish-images is the only active workflow.
- Verified live hosted evidence: runs #28 through #36 on master all concluded failure, and run #37 for commit 907679b is currently in progress.
- Next: inspect the latest failed run's jobs/logs so the story-26 plan cites the real failing stage rather than stale prior assumptions.

- Queried the latest failed hosted run (#36): validate-fast and validate-long both passed, all publish-image matrix jobs passed, and only quay-security-gate failed before publish-manifest.
- Downloaded the published image-ref artifacts and confirmed the gate is reading Quay refs like quay.io/cockroach_migrate_tool/runner:<sha>-amd64 and quay.io/cockroach_migrate_tool/verify:<sha>-amd64.
- Next: finish one more Quay API reality check, then write the story-26 plan file that captures the current hosted failure mode and the TDD execution slices.

- Created the new story-26 execution plan at .ralph/tasks/story-26-hosted-workflow-failure-investigation/01-task-debug-hosted-github-workflow-failures-parallelize-image-builds-and-surface-quay-security-findings_plans/2026-04-20-hosted-quay-gate-and-parallel-build-plan.md and linked it from the task markdown.
- Captured the real hosted failure mode from publish-images run #36: validate-fast and validate-long both passed, all publish-image matrix jobs passed, and quay-security-gate failed because it assumes the pushed per-platform Quay tag is not a manifest list even though Quay reports is_manifest_list=true and security status=queued for the returned digest.
- Next on resume: start from Slice 0 of the new plan, re-check the latest hosted master run first, then execute the workflow-only red/green loop online without adding local workflow tests or running make lanes for this task.

- Resumed story-26 task execution and confirmed the task plan exists and ends with NOW EXECUTE.
- Read the task markdown, the execution plan, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skills from disk.
- Next: re-check the latest hosted publish-images run on master before editing the workflow, because the plan requires switching back to TO BE VERIFIED if the live failure mode changed.

- Re-read .github/workflows/AGENTS.md and confirmed this task must be verified online only; local make/test lanes are explicitly forbidden for workflow correctness.
- Queried the latest publish-images runs via the authenticated GitHub API wrapper. Run #37 is still the newest run and has not yet superseded the previously recorded #36 failure.
- Next: inspect run #37 jobs directly; if it is still in progress or unchanged, treat the run #36 Quay gate failure as the active hosted failure and proceed with the narrow workflow fix.

- Re-verified the active hosted failure against run #36 logs: validate-fast and validate-long both passed, all publish-image matrix jobs succeeded, quay-security-gate failed on the hard-coded `test "${is_manifest_list}" = "false"`, and publish-manifest was skipped.
- Patched .github/workflows/publish-images.yml to start raw publish-image jobs without waiting on validation, make quay-security-gate wait on both validations plus publish-image, descend through manifest-list children instead of assuming non-list digests, classify Quay failures explicitly, and add always-run workflow summaries.
- Next: fix any actionlint findings, then commit/push and inspect the next hosted master run to verify the new workflow topology and Quay reporting online.

- actionlint passed on .github/workflows/publish-images.yml, so I committed the workflow-only change as 28bccf0 and pushed it to master to get real hosted verification.
- Left the Ralph/task bookkeeping files uncommitted on purpose; task completion state should only move after the hosted workflow proves the fix.
- Next: inspect the new publish-images run for 28bccf0, verify raw publish-image jobs start alongside validation, and read the Quay gate summary/output from the hosted logs.

- Hosted run #38 on commit 28bccf0 proved the topology change online: validate-fast, validate-long, and all publish-image jobs all started within one second of each other, all six publish-image jobs completed before validation finished, and quay-security-gate did not start until both validation jobs had passed.
- While monitoring run #38, validate-fast completed successfully at 15:06:40Z and validate-long completed successfully at 15:15:27Z; the gate then started immediately at 15:15:29Z.
- Next: verify the gate's actual Quay classification outcome and the workflow-summary job output, then decide whether this task is fully complete or whether a follow-on bug is required for residual external scan-latency behavior.

- Live Quay inspection on run #38 showed a second boundary issue: five of six new scan targets were still `queued`, so the sequential per-target polling could stretch into near-hour behavior even when scans were gradually becoming ready.
- Refactored the gate to collect all scan targets first and poll them round-robin under one global timeout, then committed/pushed that workflow follow-up as 4852f61.
- Next: verify the newest hosted run for 4852f61, confirm the same parallel topology, and inspect whether the Quay gate now reaches a clear pass/fail outcome within one global polling window.
